hypothesis.H1: Swarm behavior is self-similar across lane/session/domain scales (same Gini/CV shape)
hypothesis.H0: Inequality diverges across scales — different mechanisms at each level

method: Extract Gini coefficients and CV at three scales: (1) lane lifespan from SWARM-LANES.md, (2) lessons-per-session from lesson headers, (3) lessons-per-domain from lesson headers. Cross-scale consistency test: CV-of-Ginis < 0.3 = self-similar. Additionally: Zipf rank-size regression at each scale.

lane_scale.note: Degenerate — all lanes have 1-session lifespan (F-STR2 constraint)

session_scale.note: Moderate inequality, NOT power-law — bounded by context window

domain_scale.note: Strong power law (Zipf). Scale-free concentration — meta=157L, median=2L

interpretation: Inequality AMPLIFIES monotonically with scale (Gini: 0→0.42→0.64). Three different mechanisms produce three different distributional shapes: (1) Lane level is protocol-constrained (F-STR2 execute-or-abandon produces unit lifespan), (2) Session level is bounded by context window and human cadence (log-normal-like), (3) Domain level is unconstrained preferential attachment (Zipf α=1.1, R²=0.95 — meta gets more because meta already has more). The swarm is NOT a fractal in the self-similarity sense. It IS scale-free at the domain level specifically — meaning domain inequality is a structural property, not a policy failure.
